FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Namespace FluentGpu.Text

Classes

EditDocument

The text-edit document: a gap buffer of UTF-16 code units (grow by doubling, minimum capacity 64). Every public index is a UTF-16 code-unit offset — the unit WinUI exposes through SelectionStart/MaxLength — so the control layer never converts. Grapheme safety is layered ON TOP via the boundary scanners (SnapToGrapheme(int)/NextGrapheme(int)/PrevGrapheme(int), BCL UAX#29 segmentation — covers surrogate pairs, combining marks, ZWJ emoji, regional indicators): callers that move a caret must land on those boundaries, while the raw Insert(int, ReadOnlySpan<char>)/Remove(int, int) primitives deliberately accept any code-unit range so undo replay can restore exact historic states.

Hard line breaks are a single '\r' (WinUI normalizes CRLF/LF on the way in — see NormalizeNewlines(string)); '\n' must never be stored. The boundary scanners exploit that invariant: a position right after '\r' is always a grapheme boundary (UAX#29 GB4: break after a control), so they anchor their forward walk at the surrounding line start instead of offset 0 — O(line length), and text fields are short.

LineBreaker

THE single source of truth for line breaking, ellipsis fitting, and wrapped-box measurement — extracted verbatim from the D3D12 GlyphRenderer shaping so the layout MEASURE path and the glyph RENDER path can never disagree on where lines break or how tall a wrapped block is (the GDI-measure/DWrite-render mismatch that overlapped wrapped content). Pure, COM-free, allocation-free over a ReadOnlySpan<T> + a struct IAdvanceSource. wrap uses the TextWrap enum value (0 = NoWrap, 1 = Wrap, 2 = WrapWholeWords); trim uses the TextTrim enum value (0 = None). LTR design-advance positioning (matches the v1 renderer).

TextEditCore

The portable text-editing state machine: an EditDocument + caret/selection + bounded undo, with WinUI TextBox semantics made exact. Pure C# over the BCL — no PAL/Scene/Render types — so the same core drives TextBox/PasswordBox/NumberBox/AutoSuggestBox/ComboBox on Windows AND headless checks.

Contracts every mutation upholds: caret positions are grapheme boundaries (never inside a surrogate pair, ZWJ emoji, or combining sequence); Anchor/Active stay in [0, Length]; every document change bumps Doc.Version; undo/redo restore the EXACT before/after caret+anchor pairs. Caret movement and selection ops allocate nothing — strings materialize only at the user-gesture edge (copy, undo capture, paste normalization).

TextEditKeymap

The WinUI TextBox key map as one pure function — no state, so it is trivially testable and shared by every editable control. (None, false) means "not ours": the key must BUBBLE, which is load-bearing for Up/Down/PageUp/PageDown in single-line fields (AutoSuggestBox list navigation rides the unhandled key).

TextSeam

Process-default IFontSystem for engine layers that have no host seam to receive an instance through — concretely the input dispatcher's read-only text-selection/hyperlink gestures (rtb-02), which need the editor queries but are constructed by hosts (AppHost) that predate them. Set by each backend's constructor; last-constructed wins (the established InputHooks.Current channel-default convention). Consumers prefer an explicitly-wired instance and fall back here.

TextUndoStack

Bounded undo/redo for the text editor (cap 512 ops, oldest dropped — a hard memory ceiling per field, like RichEdit). Coalescing makes one undo step out of a typing burst: a new op merges into the open top op when the kinds match AND the positions are contiguous (typing extends forward from the previous insert's end; backspace walks backward, forward-delete stays in place) AND no selection was replaced AND the caller did not seal. Paste, IME commit, selection-replacing edits, and any caret relocation seal the run (SealRun() or sealRun: true on Push(in TextUndoOp, bool)) so they become their own undo steps — the WinUI/RichEdit feel: "abc" undoes in one step, a paste in its own step.

Structs

BreakOpp

A UAX #14 line-break opportunity for one UTF-16 position: the break condition before/after the unit (0 = Neutral, 1 = CanBreak, 2 = MayNotBreak, 3 = MustBreak) and whether it is whitespace.

GlyphPlacement

One shaped glyph in logical→visual order: the POST-shaping glyph id (NOT a codepoint), its DIP advance, the pen offset (DIP), and the first source text index it maps to (its cluster start, for wrap/hit-test/selection). Produced by the shaper (text.md §4.4); consumed by the layout engine (wrap/measure) and the glyph rasterizer.

ItemRun

One itemized run: a maximal span of the text with a single BiDi level AND a single script — the unit the shaper consumes (one run = one face/script/direction). Produced by intersecting the BiDi-level runs with the script runs. ScriptId/ScriptShapes are the opaque DirectWrite script id + shaping flags, carried as portable scalars so this seam stays Windows/COM-free.

TextKeyBinding

A resolved key chord: the command plus whether Shift extends the selection while it executes.

TextMetrics

Result of measuring a run: the box the shaped glyphs occupy + the baseline offset from the top, plus the face's decoration metrics — all vertical values measured DOWN from the line top, the same frame as Baseline. UnderlineY/UnderlineThickness place the underline bar and StrikeY the strikethrough bar (it reuses the underline thickness). These feed text decorations; backends without face metrics (the experimental GDI path) leave them 0.

TextStyle

The resolved style a text run is measured/shaped under. Weight is the NUMERIC font weight (WinUI FontWeight — the int IS the DWRITE_FONT_WEIGHT, 1..999; callers resolve sugar like Bold→700 before constructing; 0 is treated as 400). CharSpacing is WinUI TextElement.CharacterSpacing — tracking in 1/1000 em, applied per glyph after shaping. LineHeight (DIP; NaN/≤0 = font-natural) resolves per Stacking (WinUI LineStackingStrategy), and LineBounds (WinUI TextLineBounds) selects the reported line box (Full = ascent..descent, Tight = cap-height..baseline). Record equality over ALL fields is load-bearing: TextStyle is the TextMeasureCache key, so every layout-affecting input must live here.

<p><xref href="FluentGpu.Text.TextStyle.SpanRunId" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> (0 = plain single-style run) keys a <xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.SpanRunTable.Shared" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> run whose
per-range <xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.SpanStyle" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> overlays (weight/size/family/color/decorations) apply over this base style — the
rtb-01 inline-run model: the WHOLE text still shapes as ONE flow (one wrap pass), exactly like a WinUI paragraph's
inline collection. Carried here (not as a separate seam argument) so every existing layout/measure/query path —
and every cache keyed on TextStyle — is span-aware without signature churn; a span-content change mints a fresh
id, so the measure cache and the shaped-run cache self-invalidate.</p>
TextUndoOp

One reversible edit against an EditDocument: the op replaced Removed with Inserted at Start. Undo = remove Inserted.Length at Start, re-insert Removed, restore AnchorBefore/ActiveBefore; redo is the inverse with the After pair — so undo/redo restore the EXACT caret/selection the user saw, not a recomputed approximation.

Interfaces

IAdvanceSource

A per-glyph advance source for the shared wrap/measure math. Implemented as a struct so the generic wrap routines below monomorphize and inline the advance lookup (zero allocation, no virtual dispatch). The advance is the DIP advance for one UTF-16 code unit (design advance × size/em). The render side (D3D12 GlyphRenderer) and the measure side (DirectWriteFontSystem) each supply their own struct over the SAME DirectWrite design advances, so measurement and rendering break lines identically.

IFontSystem

Text seam. DirectWrite implements it on Windows; Text.Headless provides a deterministic stub for tests. The shaper resolves BiDi/script/fallback and returns FINAL device-space glyph positions in visual order; the renderer treats glyph runs as opaque positioned quads. Beyond measurement, the seam answers the editor queries — hit-test, caret geometry, selection rects — from the SAME layout pipeline (identical wrapping, identical advances), so hit-testing matches rendering exactly. For all members maxWidth is the wrap width (PositiveInfinity = no wrap), interpreted exactly as Measure(StringId, in TextStyle, float) interprets it.

ITextItemizer

Text itemization seam (text.md §4.4): segment a string into BiDi × script runs and compute per-position line-break opportunities (UAX #14). The DirectWrite leaf implements it over IDWriteTextAnalyzer + the callee IDWriteTextAnalysisSource/Sink CCWs; a portable Unicode itemizer is the cross-platform follow-up. The shaper (and, later, the layout engine's wrap) consume this output so measure and render segment identically.

Enums

TextEditCommand

Editor commands in DOCUMENT terms. Apply(TextEditCommand, bool) handles every document-order command; the vertical/visual ones (Up/Down/Page*) and the seam-touching ones (Copy/Cut/Paste need the clipboard PAL, Commit/Cancel are control semantics) intentionally fall through to the control layer.

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
